column29: The 1969 AFL Championship Game, held on January 4, 1970, at the Oakland-Alameda County Coliseum, featured the Oakland Raiders facing off against the Kansas City Chiefs. The Chiefs emerged victorious with a final score of 17-7, despite being the underdogs. Key plays included a 66-yard drive by the Chiefs to tie the game and a touchdown by Charlie Smith for the Raiders. Key players in the game were Daryle Lamonica and Warren Wells.
